Found in setup_task.sh which initializes the R script and export_result.sh which checks it.
Ran an R script with `itemFrequency(Groceries)` which confirmed 'whole milk' is the most frequent item (count=2513).
Ran apriori algorithm on Groceries data with parameters (support=0.001, confidence=0.5) which yielded 5668 rules. The task requests saving top 50 rules, making 10 a safe minimum.
The task specifies 'arules' package to load 'Groceries' dataset, which was tested and required for apriori function.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the 'Groceries' dataset, a real-world collection of point-of-sale transactions from a grocery outlet (non-synthetic). It uses the 'arules' package to identify association rules using the apriori algorithm. With the requested parameters (support = 0.001, confidence = 0.5), the algorithm generates 5668 rules, safely exceeding the min_rules_count of 10. The item frequency analysis correctly identifies 'whole milk' as the top item. All requested file outputs, such as the R script (/home/ga/RProjects/market_basket_analysis.R), directory structure, and expected CSV/PNG names perfectly match the setup, export, and verifier scripts.
